The **1987–88 NCAA Division I men's ice hockey season** began in October 1987 and concluded with the [1988 NCAA Division I Men's Ice Hockey Tournament](/wiki/1988_NCAA_Division_I_Men%27s_Ice_Hockey_Tournament "1988 NCAA Division I Men's Ice Hockey Tournament")'s championship game on April 2, 1988 at the [Olympic Center](/wiki/Herb_Brooks_Arena "Herb Brooks Arena") in [Lake Placid, New York](/wiki/Lake_Placid,_New_York "Lake Placid, New York"). This was the 41st [season](/wiki/List_of_NCAA_Division_I_men%27s_ice_hockey_seasons "List of NCAA Division I men's ice hockey seasons") in which an NCAA ice hockey championship was held and is the 94th year overall where an NCAA school fielded a team.

After the season [U.S. International](/wiki/Alliant_International_University "Alliant International University") would drop its hockey program (the school itself would eventually go bankrupt) causing the demise of the [Great West Hockey Conference](/wiki/Great_West_Hockey_Conference "Great West Hockey Conference"), the only principally west-coast conference in the history of Division I hockey.
